title: SE Conversation Rubric (v2.1)
createdBy: Nathan Hartman, with Pierce Watkins, Sjoshuan, and Jamie

scoring.essentialPolicy: All Essentials must score green to be considered successful Street Epistemology. A grey or red on any Essential disqualifies the conversation as SE.

sections.title: Getting Consent, Identifying and Clarifying the Claim, & Confidence Level

criteria.recommended: Obtains informed consent to have the conversation
criteria.detrimental: Forces the conversation on or deceives the conversation partner regarding the nature of the conversation

criteria.recommended: Settles on an important or interesting fact-based or normative claim
criteria.detrimental: Settles on a trivial, obvious, or personal preference based claim

criteria.recommended: Asks for confidence using a scale (can be numeric or verbal)
criteria.detrimental: Imposes a specific confidence scale, even when the conversation partner is uncomfortable with it

criteria.recommended: Asks for clarification of terms
criteria.detrimental: Imposes their own definitions rather than using their conversation partner's definitions

criteria.recommended: Asks why their conversation partner is at their stated confidence level
criteria.detrimental: Assumes the reasons why their conversation partner is at their stated confidence level

criteria.recommended: Utilizes real-reason checks to confirm that reasons given support confidence
criteria.detrimental: Wastes time and energy critically examining reasons that do not support confidence

criteria.recommended: Asks questions related to how their conversation partner forms beliefs
criteria.detrimental: Exclusively asks questions related to what and why, but not how

criteria.recommended: Asks their conversation partner to generate alternative explanations
criteria.detrimental: Provides alternative explanations unprompted

criteria.recommended: Asks if a reason is relevant to the claim
criteria.detrimental: Tells their conversation partner that their reason is irrelevant to the claim

criteria.recommended: Asks if a reason is strong enough to support the confidence level
criteria.detrimental: Tells their conversation partner that their reason is not strong enough to support their confidence level

criteria.recommended: Asks if the premises supporting a claim or reason are true
criteria.detrimental: Tells their conversation partner that the premises supporting their claim or reason are not true

criteria.recommended: Asks about the testability of the claim or reasons
criteria.detrimental: Tells their conversation partner that their claim or reasons are untestable

criteria.recommended: Asks what could increase and/or decrease the conversation partner's confidence
criteria.detrimental: Tells their conversation partner what should increase and/or decrease their confidence

criteria.recommended: Asks if anything could show the belief to be false IF it is false
criteria.detrimental: Asserts that their conversation partner has no way to show the belief to be false if it is false

criteria.recommended: Asks if someone else could use the same reason(s) for a different belief (outsider test)
criteria.detrimental: Asserts that other people use the same reasoning to believe things that are false

criteria.recommended: Explores how their conversation partner conceptualizes and values truth if appropriate
criteria.detrimental: Allows relativistic thinking to go un-challenged

criteria.recommended: Attempts to understand the underlying reasons and methods behind their interlocutor's beliefs
criteria.detrimental: Assumes the underlying reasons and methods behind their conversation partner's beliefs

criteria.recommended: Asks mostly open-ended, thought-provoking questions utilizing the Socratic method
criteria.detrimental: Asks many closed-ended or leading questions

criteria.recommended: Encourages deep thinking and self-reflection, especially regarding the quality of reasoning
criteria.detrimental: Asks only surface level questions or otherwise fails to investigate the conversation partner's epistemology

criteria.recommended: Listens carefully and repeats back to avoid misrepresenting their conversation partner
criteria.detrimental: Misrepresents or strawmans their conversation partner

criteria.recommended: Puts the burden for understanding and rapport on themselves
criteria.detrimental: Puts the burden for communicating clearly and maintaining rapport on their conversation partner

criteria.recommended: Controls their body language and tone
criteria.detrimental: Communicates negative dispositions or attitudes through body language and/or tone

criteria.recommended: Shows appreciation or offers praise to their conversation partner
criteria.detrimental: Expresses disappointment or judgment toward their conversation partner

criteria.recommended: Provides their opinion or view when the interlocutor asks for it (or at the end of the conversation)
criteria.detrimental: Shares their own unsolicited opinions or imposes their own view

criteria.recommended: Uses neutral terms and avoids common triggers
criteria.detrimental: Uses sensitive or offensive language

criteria.recommended: Uses common language and layman's terms where possible
criteria.detrimental: Uses unnecessary jargon and technical terminology

criteria.recommended: Avoids interrupting or only interrupts when necessary to keep the conversation on track
criteria.detrimental: Interrupts frequently or when it is inappropriate

criteria.recommended: Asks clear, concise, and easily understood questions
criteria.detrimental: Asks long, complicated, or confusing questions

criteria.recommended: Moves quickly enough to avoid boredom, but not so fast as to be overwhelming
criteria.detrimental: Progresses the conversation too quickly or too slowly

criteria.recommended: Respects their conversation partner's time
criteria.detrimental: Shows a lack of respect for their conversation partner's time

criteria.recommended: Asks if their conversation partner's confidence has changed
criteria.detrimental: Tells their conversation partner that they should change their confidence

criteria.recommended: Asks for their conversation partner's opinion on how the conversation went
criteria.detrimental: Tells their conversation partner how the conversation went

criteria.recommended: Offers their conversation partner an opportunity to ask them questions
criteria.detrimental: Refuses to answer questions posed by their conversation partner

criteria.recommended: Used collaborative language throughout the conversation
criteria.detrimental: Used confrontational or combative language

criteria.recommended: Treated their conversation partner charitably throughout the conversation
criteria.detrimental: Treated their conversation partner uncharitably

criteria.recommended: Appeared to have the goal of exploring beliefs rather than changing them directly
criteria.detrimental: Appeared to have the goal of changing their interlocutor's beliefs directly rather than exploring their beliefs

criteria.recommended: Maintained civility throughout the conversation
criteria.detrimental: Conversation deteriorated to the point where it ends on poor terms
